revision.text:
'''大物忌神'''（おおものいみのかみ）は、[[鳥海山]]に宿るとされる[[神 (神道)|神]]である。鳥海山頂の[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]をはじめ、[[東北地方]]各地に大物忌神を祀る[[神社]]がある。
鳥海山は古代の[[ヤマト王権]]の支配圏の北辺にあることから、大物忌神は国家を守る神とされ、また、[[穢れ]]を清める神ともされた。鳥海山は[[火山]]であり、鳥海山の噴火は大物忌神の怒りであると考えられ、噴火のたびにより高い[[神階]]が授けられた。
大物忌神は、[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]・大忌神・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]などと同神とされる。鳥海山大物忌神社の社伝では神宮外宮の豊受大神と同神としている。
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:日本の神|おおものいみのかみ]]

'''大物忌神'''（おおものいみのかみ）は、[[鳥海山]]に宿るとされる[[神 (神道)|神]]である。鳥海山頂の[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]をはじめ、[[東北地方]]各地に大物忌神を祀る[[神社]]がある。
鳥海山は古代の[[ヤマト王権]]の支配圏の北辺にあることから、大物忌神は国家を守る神とされ、また、[[穢れ]]を清める神ともされた。鳥海山は[[火山]]であり、鳥海山の噴火は大物忌神の怒りであると考えられ、噴火のたびにより高い[[神階]]が授けられた。
大物忌神は、[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]・大忌神・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]などと同神とされる。鳥海山大物忌神社の社伝では神宮外宮の豊受大神と同神としている。[[鳥海月山両所宮]]では鳥海山の神として倉稲魂命を祀っている。
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:日本の神|おおものいみのかみ]]

{{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}

== 概要 == 鳥海山は古代の[[ヤマト王権]]の支配圏の北辺にあることから、大物忌神は国家を守る神とされ、また、[[穢れ]]を清める神ともされた。鳥海山は[[火山]]であり、鳥海山の噴火は大物忌神の怒りであると考えられ、噴火のたびにより高い[[神階]]が授けられた。
大物忌神は、[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]・大忌神・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]などと同神とされる。鳥海山大物忌神社の社伝では神宮外宮の豊受大神と同神としている。
[[鳥海月山両所宮]]では鳥海山の神として倉稲魂命を祀っている。
[[手長足長]]の悪事を見かね霊鳥である三本足の[[カラス|鴉]]を遣わせ、手長足長が現れるときには「有や」現れないときには「無や」と鳴かせて人々に知らせるようにした。山のふもとの[[三崎公園 (秋田県・山形県)|三崎峠]]が「有耶無耶の関」と呼ばれるのはこれが由来とされる<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|work=[http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/ 湧水の里 遊佐町]|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課|accessdate=2009-01-31}}</ref>。

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/91046908|大物忌神]]"
revision.text:
[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Ōmonoimi-jinja|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]]
== overview == Mt. Chokai is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified [[kegare]]. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is believed to be the same deity as several other kami, including Kurinama no Mikoto, Toyouke no Okami, Oimimi no kami, and Hirose no kami. The Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine's tradition states that this deity is identical to Toyouke Omikami at Jingu Geku.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Kurinama no Mikoto]], [[Toyoukebime|Toyouke no Okami]], Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the company tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a {{Ill|three legged raven|ja|手長足長}}, a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|access-date=2009-01-31|website=[http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/ 湧水の里 遊佐町]|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
== Sources == {{Reflist}}
* [[Category:Gods and goddesses of Japan]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Omonoiminokami]] to [[Omonoimi no Kami]]

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Ōmonoimi-jinja|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]]
== overview == Mt. Chokai is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified [[kegare]]. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is believed to be the same deity as several other kami, including Kurinama no Mikoto, Toyouke no Okami, Oimimi no kami, and Hirose no kami. The Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine's tradition states that this deity is identical to Toyouke Omikami at Jingu Geku.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Kurinama no Mikoto]], [[Toyoukebime|Toyouke no Okami]], Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[three legged crow]], a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|access-date=2009-01-31|website=[http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/ 湧水の里 遊佐町]|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Ōmonoimi-jinja|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]]
== overview == Mt. Chokai is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified [[kegare]]. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is believed to be the same deity as several other kami, including Kurinama no Mikoto, Toyouke no Okami, Oimimi no kami, and Hirose no kami. The Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine's tradition states that this deity is identical to Toyouke Omikami at Jingu Geku.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyoukebime|Toyouke no Okami]], Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the company tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[three legged crow]], a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|access-date=2009-01-31|website=[http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/ 湧水の里 遊佐町]|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . {{Nihongo||大物忌神|{{ill|Omonoimi no Kami|lt=Ōmonoimi-no-kami|ja|大物忌神|simple}}}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref>{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyoukebime|Toyouke no Okami]], Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|access-date=2009-01-31|website=[http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/ 湧水の里 遊佐町]|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課|archive-date=2007-12-18|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref>

== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

revision.text:
[[File:Mount Chōkai (2017-05-19) - Flickr.jpg|thumb|300px|鳥海山]] '''大物忌神'''（おおものいみのかみ）は、[[山形県]]の[[鳥海山]]に宿るとされる[[神 (神道)|神]]である。鳥海山頂の[[鳥海山大物忌神社]]をはじめ、[[東北地方]]各地に大物忌神を祀る[[神社]]がある。
== 概要 == 鳥海山は古代の[[ヤマト王権]]の支配圏の北辺にあることから、大物忌神は国家を守る神とされ、また、[[穢れ]]を清める神ともされた。鳥海山は[[火山]]であり、鳥海山の噴火は大物忌神の怒りであると考えられ、噴火のたびにより高い[[神階]]が授けられた。
大物忌神は、[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[トヨウケビメ|豊受大神]]・大忌神・[[廣瀬大社|広瀬神]]などと同神とされる。鳥海山大物忌神社の社伝では神宮外宮の豊受大神と同神としている。
[[鳥海月山両所宮]]では鳥海山の神として倉稲魂命を祀っている。
[[手長足長]]の悪事を見かね霊鳥である三本足の[[カラス|鴉]]を遣わせ、手長足長が現れるときには「有や」現れないときには「無や」と鳴かせて人々に知らせるようにした。山のふもとの[[三崎公園 (秋田県・山形県)|三崎峠]]が「有耶無耶の関」と呼ばれるのはこれが由来とされる<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|work=[http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/ 湧水の里 遊佐町]|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課|accessdate=2009-01-31}}</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
==関連項目== * [[鳥海山]]
{{Shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおものいみのかみ}} [[Category:日本の神|おおものいみのかみ]] [[Category:食物神]] [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:鳥海山]]

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Ōmonoimi-jinja|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>

[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

{{Nihongo||大物忌神|{{ill|Omonoimi no Kami|lt=Ōmonoimi-no-kami|ja|大物忌神|simple}}}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|{{ill|Omonoimi no Kami|lt=Ōmonoimi-no-kami|ja|大物忌神|simple}}}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}
== References == {{Reflist}}

[[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[en:Omonoimi no Kami]]

According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|{{ill|Omonoimi no Kami|lt=Ōmonoimi-no-kami|ja|大物忌神|simple}}}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

revision.text:
[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Ill|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Ill|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}
