This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Toyouke-hime|en|4=ja|5=トヨウケビメ}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[Mount Chōkai]] in [[Yamagata prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|kegare|en}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Shrine|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai gassan ryōsho-gu|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[Mount Chōkai]] erupted his rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

revision.text:
[[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3270544]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi no Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[:en:Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[:en:Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3270544]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi no Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[:en:Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[:en:Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

There are [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[:en:Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>

== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3270544]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi no Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]] . There are [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrines]] that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places [[:en:Tōhoku region|in the Tohoku region]], including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]], and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke-hime]]{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] in [[:en:Yamagata Prefecture|Yamagata Prefecture]], or the northernmost post of the land of [[:en:Yamato period|Yamato]].}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], [[:en:Toyouke-hime|Toyouke no Okami]],<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a [[:en:Yatagarasu|three legged crow]], a sacred [[:en:Crow|crow]], was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time [[:en:Mount Chōkai|Mount Chōkai]] erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] {{Infobox deity}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q3270544]] Wikidata name: Omonoimi no Kami [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}} . There are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines}} that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=in the Tohoku region}}, including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}}, and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture}}, or the northernmost post of the land of {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato}}.}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Okami}},<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=three legged crow}}, a sacred {{ill|Crow|en|Crow|WD=Q388450|lt=crow}}, was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai}} erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Omonoimi no Kami]][[ja:大物忌神]][[simple:Omonoimi no Kami]][[zh:大物忌神]] {{Infobox deity}} [[File:Mount_Chōkai_(2017-05-19)_-_Flickr.jpg|thumb|300x300px| Mt. Chokai]] '''Omonoiminokami (大物忌神)''' is [[Kami|a god]] who is said to reside on {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}} . There are {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrines|1=Shinto shrine}} that enshrine Omonoiminokami in various places {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=in the Tohoku region|1=Tōhoku region}}, including [[Chōkaisan Ōmonoimi Shrine|Chokaisan '''Ōmonoimi''' Shrine]] on the summit of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}}, and [[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū]].<ref>{{Cite web|title=鳥海月山両所宮|url=http://www.komainu.org/yamagata/yamagatasi/ryousyo/ryousyo.html|access-date=2023-04-12|website=www.komainu.org}}</ref>
{{Nihongo||大物忌神|Ōmonoimi-no-kami}} is considered possibly identical to {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime|1=Toyouke-hime}}{{efn|Ōmonoimi-no-kami is believed to be the god of {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} in {{ill|Yamagata Prefecture|en|Yamagata Prefecture|WD=Q125863|lt=Yamagata Prefecture|1=Yamagata Prefecture}}, or the northernmost post of the land of {{ill|Yamato period|en|Yamato period|WD=Q633881|lt=Yamato|1=Yamato period}}.}} <ref name=":0">{{cite book|title=Nihon no kamigami no jiten : Shinto saishi to yaoyorozu no kamigami|author1=Sonoda, Minoru|author2=Mogi, Sakae|publisher=[[:ja:学研ホールディングス|Gakken]]|year=1997|isbn=9784056016291|series=Books esoterica, 2.; New sight mook|pages=68, 69|language=ja|oclc=42978057|ref={{sfnref|Sonoda and Mogi|1997}}|author-link=:ja:薗田稔}}</ref>
== Overview == {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} is in the northern area of Yamato sovereignty. The people there believed that Daimotsui no kami protected their nation and purified {{ill|kegare|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kegare|qq=}}. The mountain is a volcano, and there have been eruptions in the past.
Omonoimimi no kami is considered to be the same deity as [[Ukanomitama|Uka no mitama]], {{ill|Toyouke-hime|en|Toyouke-hime|WD=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke no Okami|1=Toyouke-hime}},<ref name=":0" /> Oimimi no kami, and [[Hirose Taisha|Hirose no kami]]. According to the shrine tradition of Chokaisan Daimotsui-jinja Shrine, it is the same deity as Toyouke Omikami of Jingu Geku.
[[Chōkai Gassan Ryōsho-gū|Chokai Gassan Ryoshonomiya]] enshrines Kurinama no Mikoto as the deity of Mt. Chokai.
According to legend, a {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=three legged crow|1=Yatagarasu}}, a sacred {{ill|Crow|en|Crow|WD=Q388450|lt=crow|1=Crow}}, was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}

According to legend, a {{ill|Yatagarasu|en|Yatagarasu|WD=Q745463|lt=three legged crow|1=Yatagarasu}}, a sacred {{ill|Crow|en|Crow|WD=Q388450|lt=crow|1=Crow}}, was sent to observe the evil deeds of a being with long arms. The raven would make a sound of "Yes" to signal the appearance of the being and "Muya" when it did not appear. This is said to be why the Misaki Pass at the base of the mountain is called "Yuyamuya no Seki".<ref name="yuza">{{Cite web|title=三崎公園・有耶無耶の関|url=http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20071218230715/http://www.town.yuza.yamagata.jp/Files/1/6007/html/%E4%B8%89%E5%B4%8E%E5%85%AC%E5%9C%92.html|archive-date=2007-12-18|access-date=2009-01-31|website=town.yuza.yamagata.jp|publisher=[[遊佐町]]産業振興課}}</ref>
Every time {{ill|Mount Chōkai|en|Mount Chōkai|WD=Q713826|lt=Mount Chōkai|1=Mount Chōkai}} erupted his Rank increased<ref>{{Cite web|title=Tallest Peak in North Japan: The Shugen History of Chokai-san|url=https://timbunting.com/mountains/chokai/|access-date=2023-05-10|website=Tim Bunting|language=en-NZ}}</ref> ==Notelist== {{notelist}}
